# Setting up your team

This guide explains how new members join and what roles they take on.

## Onboarding a new engineer

When a new engineer joins, he should be assigned a buddy from the existing team. His buddy is responsible for walking him through the codebase during his first week. Make sure he completes the setup checklist before his first standup.

If the engineer is more senior, she may be asked to lead onboarding sessions herself. In that case, her schedule should be adjusted to accommodate the extra meetings.

## Support team structure

Our support team is run by a small group of firemen and first responders who handle incidents as they come in. Each shift is managed by a team chairman who coordinates escalations.

If no one is available, the on-call policeman will receive the page.

## Culture and performance

We hire ninja engineers who can crush the competition and move fast. Rockstar developers are especially valued on our infrastructure team, where you need to be able to hit the ground running and dominate performance benchmarks.

If you're not a killer coder, this may not be the right environment for you.

## Escalation policy

When an incident is escalated, the on-call engineer must man the incident channel until it's resolved. Every man on the team is expected to be reachable during their on-call shift.
